title: Guhyakālī Sādhana
subcategory: Śākta-Kālī — Secret / Hidden Kālī (Guhyakālī Khanda of Mahākāla Saṃhitā)
century: Medieval (c. 10th–14th c.); digest editions c. 19th–20th c.
description: Guhyakālī ("the Secret Kālī") is the esoteric form of Kālī documented in the *Guhyakālī Khaṇḍa* of the *Mahākāla Saṃhitā* — a many-faced, many-armed goddess manifesting in forms with one to one hundred faces, the principal *daśavaktra* (ten-faced) form having 27 eyes and 54 arms, including human, animal, and bird faces. High caution — the most secret and elaborate of the Kālī-Krama practices. Pres
